standards.statement: Recognize the five senses and related body parts. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Recognize the senses of sight, hearing, and smell and related body parts.
Recognize the senses of sight and hearing and related body parts.
Recognize and respond to one type of sensory stimuli.

Recognize that some books and other media portray animals and plants with characteristics and behaviors they do not have in real life. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify a behavior of an animal or plant in a book or other media that is not real.
Distinguish a real animal and an animal that is not a living thing, such as a toy animal.
Distinguish between a plant and animal.

Observe plants and animals, describe how they are alike and how they are different in the way they look and in the things they do. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify differences in characteristics of plants and animals.
Match identical animals and plants.
Observe that things that make sound vibrate. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify objects that create specific sounds.
Match sounds to specific objects.
Recognize and respond to common sounds.
Investigate that things move in different ways, such as fast, slow, etc. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Identify ways that things move, such as fast or slow.
Recognize that things move.
Track objects in motion.
Observe that a push or a pull can change the way an object is moving. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Demonstrate pushing or pulling of an object to make it move.
Recognize that pushing or pulling an object makes it move.
Track the movement of objects that are pushed or pulled.

Sort objects by observable properties, such as size, shape, color, temperature (hot or cold), weight (heavy or light) and texture. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Sort objects by observable properties, such as size, shape, or color.
Match objects by an observable property, such as size or color.
Recognize two common objects that are identical to each other.
Recognize that the shape of materials such as paper and clay can be changed by cutting, tearing, crumpling, smashing, or rolling. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Recognize that the shape of objects, such as paper, changes when cut, torn, or crumpled.
Recognize that the shape of objects, such as paper, changes when cut or torn.
Recognize a change in an object.

Explore the Law of Gravity by investigating how objects are pulled toward the ground unless something holds them up. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify that objects can fall to the ground unless something stops them.
Recognize that objects fall to the ground.
Track a falling object.
Recognize the repeating pattern of day and night. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify daily activities in a 24-hour period, such as eating breakfast and going to bed, and associate activities with morning and night.
Identify one common activity that occurs in the day and one that occurs in the night.
Recognize one common activity that occurs during the day.
Recognize that the Sun can only be seen in the daytime. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify the Sun in the daytime.
Recognize the Sun in the daytime.
Associate the Sun with daytime.
Observe that sometimes the Moon can be seen at night and sometimes during the day. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify the Moon in the sky at night.
Recognize the Moon in the sky at night.
Associate the Moon with night.
Observe that things can be big and things can be small as seen from Earth. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Observe big and small things in the sky.
Recognize the size of items as either big or small.
Recognize items that are big.
Observe that some objects are far away and some are nearby as seen from Earth. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Identify an item that is far away and an item that is nearby.
Recognize familiar objects that are far away or nearby.
Recognize items as nearby.
Collaborate with a partner to collect information. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify a partner to obtain information.
Collect a designated item with a partner.
Share objects with a partner.
Make observations of the natural world and know that they are descriptors collected using the five senses. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify information about objects and actions in the natural world through observation.
Identify information about objects in the natural world through observation.
Recognize common objects in the natural world through observation.
Keep records as appropriate -- such as pictorial records -- of investigations conducted. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Observe, explore, and create a visual representation of real objects.
Observe, explore, and match pictures to real objects.
Observe and create a visual representation of an object which includes its major features. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Recognize that learning can come from careful observation. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Make observations of living things and their environment using the five senses. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Use sight, hearing, and smell to make observations.
Use sight and hearing to make observations.
Recognize and respond to different types of sensory stimuli.
Identify the major parts of plants, including stem, roots, leaves, and flowers. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify the leaf, flower, and stem of a plant.
Recognize the leaf and flower of a plant.
Recognize that plants have leaves.
Differentiate between living and nonliving things. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Identify characteristics of living and nonliving things, including whether they need food or water.
Distinguish common living and nonliving things in the environment.
Recognize self and others as living things.
Make observations that plants and animals closely resemble their parents, but variations exist among individuals within a population. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Match offspring of specific animals to adult animals.
Recognize that baby plants and animals have parents.
Recognize one’s own parents.
Through observation, recognize that all plants and animals, including humans, need the basic necessities of air, water, food, and space. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Observe and recognize that plants and animals need water and food.
Observe and recognize that plants and animals need water.
Observe and recognize that people need water.

Demonstrate and describe the various ways that objects can move, such as in a straight line, zigzag, back-and-forth, round-and-round, fast, and slow. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Demonstrate and identify that objects can move in different ways, such as up and down, in a straight line, and back and forth.
Demonstrate that objects can move in different ways, such as up and down.
Track objects moving up and down.
Demonstrate that the way to change the motion of an object is by applying a push or a pull. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify the effect that a push or pull has on an object, such as changing the way an object moves.
Demonstrate and recognize that pushing or pulling of an object makes it move.
Apply a push to move an object.

Sort objects by observable properties, such as size, shape, color, temperature (hot or cold), weight (heavy or light), texture, and whether objects sink or float. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Sort objects by observable properties, such as size, shape, color, or texture.
Sort objects by an observable property, such as size, shape, or color.
Identify common classroom objects by one observable property, such as size or color.

Observe and discuss that there are more stars in the sky than anyone can easily count and that they are not scattered evenly in the sky. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Recognize that there are many stars in the sky.
Associate stars with the night sky.

Explore the Law of Gravity by demonstrating that Earth's gravity pulls any object on or near Earth toward it even though nothing is touching the object. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Observe and recognize that an object will fall when it is dropped.
Indicate the location of an object before and after it falls.
Track objects that fall to the ground.

Investigate how magnifiers make things appear bigger and help people see things they could not see without them. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify that magnifiers enlarge the appearance of objects.
Match a magnified item to its original item.
Recognize a familiar object enlarged by magnification.
Identify the beneficial and harmful properties of the Sun. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Recognize positive and harmful effects of sunlight.
Recognize a positive effect and a negative effect of sunlight.
Recognize effects of sunlight, such as warming and giving light.
Recognize that water, rocks, soil, and living organisms are found on Earth's surface. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify rocks, water, and living things in the environment.
Recognize rocks and living things in the environment.
Recognize living things in the environment.
Describe the need for water and how to be safe around water. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify reasons people need water and safe practices around water.
Identify reasons people need water.
Recognize one way people use water.
Recognize that some things in the world around us happen fast and some happen slowly. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Distinguish between events that happen slowly and those that happen fast.
Distinguish between actions that are fast or slow.
Recognize an action as fast or slow.

Raise questions about the natural world, investigate them in teams through free exploration, and generate appropriate explanations based on those explorations. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Request information about the environment.
Ask questions about common objects in the environment.
Recognize common objects in the environment.

Using the five senses as tools, make careful observations, describe objects in terms of number, shape, texture, size, weight, color, and motion, and compare their observations with others. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Use careful observation to identify objects based on size, shape, color, or texture.
Recognize differences in objects through observation of size, shape, or color
Recognize common objects as the same.
Keep records as appropriate - such as pictorial and written records - of investigations conducted. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Draw pictures about investigations conducted.
Contribute to group recordings of observations.
Ask "how do you know?" in appropriate situations. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Ask a question about a science investigation.

Distinguish human body parts (brain, heart, lungs, stomach, muscles, and skeleton) and their basic functions. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify major external body parts, such as hands and legs, and their uses.
Match external body parts, such as a foot, to their uses.
Recognize one or more external body parts.

Observe and describe major stages in the life cycles of plants and animals, including beans and butterflies. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Observe and recognize the major stages in the life cycles of plants and animals.
Observe and recognize the sequence of stages in the life cycles of common animals.
Recognize that offspring can be matched with their parents, such as a human baby with adult humans and a puppy with dogs.
Compare and contrast the basic needs that all living things, including humans, have for survival. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify the basic needs of living things, including water, food, and air.
Recognize that living things have basic needs, including water and food.
Recognize that animals need water.

Recognize and explain that living things are found all over Earth, but each is only able to live in habitats that meet its basic needs. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Recognize that many different kinds of living things are found in different habitats.
Recognize that many kinds of living things are found in the environment.
Recognize common living things in the immediate environment.
Discuss that people use electricity or other forms of energy to cook their food, cool or warm their homes, and power their cars. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify ways people use electricity in their lives.
Recognize a way people use electricity in their lives.
Activate a device that uses electricity.
Investigate the effect of applying various pushes and pulls on different objects. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Observe and identify that pushing or pulling an object can change the direction of movement of the object.
Identify that pushing or pulling an object makes it move.
Recognize that pushing and pulling an object makes it move.
Demonstrate that magnets can be used to make some things move without touching them. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Observe and recognize that magnets can move some objects.
Use magnets to cause objects to move.
Recognize that objects are pulled toward the ground unless something holds them up. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Indicate that an object has fallen.
Identify and demonstrate that an object will fall to the ground when dropped.
Recognize that an object will fall to the ground when dropped.

Demonstrate that the greater the force (push or pull) applied to an object, the greater the change in motion of the object. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify that pushing or pulling an object with more force will make the object go faster or farther.
Recognize that pushing or pulling an object with more force will make the object go faster or farther.

Observe and measure objects in terms of their properties, including size, shape, color, temperature, weight, texture, sinking or floating in water, and attraction and repulsion of magnets. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify objects by observable properties, such as, size, shape, color,
Identify objects by observable properties, such as size, shape, and color.
Match objects by one observable property, such as size or color.
Identify objects and materials as solid, liquid, or gas. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Identify objects and materials as solid or liquid.
Recognize water in solid or liquid states.
Recognize water as a liquid.
Recognize that solids have a definite shape and that liquids and gases take the shape of their container. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Recognize that solids have a definite shape and liquids take the shape of their container.
Recognize that solids have a definite shape.
Recognize different containers that hold liquids.
Observe and describe water in its solid, liquid, and gaseous states. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)
Measure and compare temperatures taken every day at the same time. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Describe and compare outside daily temperatures as warm or cold.
Identify outside temperatures as warm or cold.
Recognize common objects or materials as warm or cold.
Measure and compare the volume of liquids using containers of various shapes and sizes. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Compare the volume of liquid in a variety of containers.
Recognize different volumes of liquids in identical containers.

Investigate that materials can be altered to change some of their properties, but not all materials respond the same way to any one alteration. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Explore and identify that observable properties of materials can be changed.
Recognize changes in observable properties of materials.
Recognize that the appearance of an object or material has changed.
Recognize that Earth is made up of rocks. Rocks come in many sizes and shapes. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Sort rocks according to size and shape.
Sort rocks according to size.
Recognize the ground in the environment.

Describe how small pieces of rock and dead plant and animal parts can be the basis of soil and explain the process by which soil is formed. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Classify soil types based on color, texture (size of particles), the ability to retain water, and the ability to support the growth of plants. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Recognize soil types based on color (dark or light) and texture (size of particles).

Compare and describe changing patterns in nature that repeat themselves, such as weather conditions including temperature and precipitation, day to day and season to season. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify common weather patterns associated with each season.
Recognize types of weather and match to the weather outdoors.
Recognize daily outdoor temperature as hot or cold.

Investigate by observing and measuring, that the Sun's energy directly and indirectly warms the water, land, and air. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Recognize that items outside are heated by the Sun.

Investigate, observe and describe how water left in an open container disappears (evaporates), but water in a closed container does not disappear (evaporate). Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Recognize that wet things will dry when they are left in the air.
Investigate that air is all around us and that moving air is wind. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Identify effects of wind.
Recognize effects of wind.

Raise questions about the natural world, investigate them in teams through free exploration and systematic observations, and generate appropriate explanations based on those explorations. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Ask questions and make observations about things in the natural world.
Answer yes and no questions and make observations about common objects and actions in the natural world.
Request a change or help to solve a problem in the environment.
Compare the observations made by different groups using the same tools. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Identify information about objects based on observation.
Identify characteristics of objects based on observation.
Use senses to recognize objects.

Ask "how do you know?" in appropriate situations and attempt reasonable answers when asked the same question by others. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Explain how particular scientific investigations should yield similar conclusions when repeated. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)
Recognize that the results of a scientific activity should be the same when repeated
Recognize that science activities can be repeated.
Recognize common objects in different environments.

Distinguish between empirical observation (what you see, hear, feel, smell, or taste) and ideas or inferences (what you think). Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Explain how scientists alone or in groups are always investigating new ways to solve problems. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Recognize that scientists work to solve problems.
Recognize that people work in science.

Describe structures in plants and their roles in food production, support, water and nutrient transport, and reproduction. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)

Identify the major parts of a plant, such as the root, stem, leaf, and flower.
Recognize the leaf and flower of a plant.

Identify behaviors of plants that show they are growing.

Classify animals into major groups (mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, fish, arthropods, vertebrates and invertebrates, those having live births and those which lay eggs) according to their physical characteristics and behaviors. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts
Classify animals by a similar physical characteristic, such as fur, feathers, and number of legs.

Match animals that are the same.

Match plants that are the same.

Identify changes in the appearance of animals and plants throughout the year.

Recognize that plants use energy from the Sun, air, and water to make their own food. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)

Recognize that plants need water.
Identify some basic forms of energy such as light, heat, sound, electrical, and mechanical. Content Complexity: Level 1: Recall Related Access Point(s)

Demonstrate that light travels in a straight line until it strikes an object or travels from one medium to another. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Recognize examples of sources of light, such as the Sun or a flashlight.

Identify that light may come from different sources, such as the Sun or electric lamp.

Recognize sources of heat.
Measure and compare temperatures of various samples of solids and liquids. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Observe and identify the colder/hotter temperature measured on a thermometer.
Recognize that a thermometer measures temperature (cold and hot).
Recognize the temperature of items, such as food, as cool or warm.

Measure the weight of solids or liquids.
Sort solid objects by weight (heavy and light).
Recognize the larger of two objects.
Compare materials and objects according to properties such as size, shape, color, texture, and hardness. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Group objects by two observable properties, such as size and shape or color and texture.
Sort objects by an observable property, such as size, shape, color, and texture.
Match objects by an observable property, such as size, shape, and color.

Raise questions about the natural world, investigate them individually and in teams through free exploration and systematic investigations, and generate appropriate explanations based on those explorations. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Ask literal questions, explore, observe, and share information.

Compare the observations made by different groups using the same tools and seek reasons to explain the differences across groups. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Keep records as appropriate, such as pictorial, written, or simple charts and graphs, of investigations conducted. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Record observations to describe findings using written or visual formats, such as picture stories.

Recognize the importance of communication among scientists. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Recognize that people share information.
Recognize that scientists share their knowledge and results with each other.
Recognize that people work in different kinds of jobs related to science.
Recognize that scientists question, discuss, and check each other's evidence and explanations. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)
Infer based on observation. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Explain that empirical evidence is information, such as observations or measurements, that is used to help validate explanations of natural phenomena. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Recognize that words in science can have different or more specific meanings than their use in everyday language; for example, energy, cell, heat/cold, and evidence. Content Complexity: Level 2: Basic Application of Skills & Concepts Related Access Point(s)

Explain that although characteristics of plants and animals are inherited, some characteristics can be affected by the environment. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Recognize similarities between self and parents.
Recognize that animal behaviors may be shaped by heredity and learning. Content Complexity: Level 3: Strategic Thinking & Complex Reasoning Related Access Point(s)

Identify similarities in the major stages in the life cycles of common Florida plants and animals.
